Off Calendar Mar-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER PROTUS TANIFORM'S Motion Re: Petition Writ Of Mandate. Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling. See minutes in Word format for the Court's entire ruling. Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, (E: maria.torreano@gmail.com)

Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Added For Purposes Of Entering Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Mar-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Petition For Writ Of Mandate - Off calendar. Duplicate Entry.

Application Pending For Waiver Of Additional Court Fees And Costs Pursuant To G.C. 68633, Crc 3.51, 8.26, And 8.818
Off Calendar Feb-19-2020 Text Ruling

Order To Show Cause (Per Ex Parte Order Filed 3/15/19)
Off Calendar Apr-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jay T. Tambeck, Esq Leigh Law Group, 415-713-3683, appearing for Plaintiff;
Brett Kingsbury, Esq., Satat ofCalifornia Department of Justice, 415-510-3472, appearing for DefendantCommossion in the teacher credentialing et al

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Petitioner Protus Taniform's application to stay revocation of teaching credentials is DENIED. Petitioner has not shown that Respondent California Commission on Teacher Credentialing is unlikely to prevail on the merits. (Code Civ. Proc. 1094.5(h)(1).) Petitioner has not shown that the administrative law judge committed a prejudicial abuse of discretion in applying the Morrison factors (Morrison v. State Board of Education (1969) 1 Cal.3d 214, 229; 5 Cal. Code Regs. 80302) or that the ALJ's findings regarding those factors are not supported by the weight of the evidence. (See Broney v. California Comm'n on Teacher Credentialing (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 462, 476-479.) Because Petitioner has failed to establish the agency is unlikely to prevail, the Court need not consider the public interest criterion. (See Medical Board of California v. Superior Court (1991) 227 Cal.App.3d 1458, 1461 [trial court abused its discretion in staying administrative order revoking petitioner's license to practice as physician].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Sculman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn CSR6185, Tonyvaughn@yahoo.com (302/EPS) (see scanned order for details)
